storm.summary_layperson: Scattered showers are passing north of town and weakening — most should slide off to the east without much impact on the cove.
storm.local_effect_blurb: Cells are tracking over land north of the cove, not crossing the Sound, so the water's cooling effect doesn't really come into play today.
storm.detail_html:
Regional picture. KOKX radar shows a band of light-to-moderate cells 8–18 mi north of the cove, with the strongest core reaching 46 dBZ at about 13 mi N. NWS has no active watches, warnings, or advisories for the area. The HRRR shows essentially no instability over our region — CAPE around 20 J/kg (RAP fallback 80 J/kg), so these cells are running on fumes.

Local modifier. Water at the Sound buoy is 67°F vs air 72°F (a +5°F delta), which would normally set up a weak stable marine layer over the Sound. But today's cells are approaching from the north over land, not from the southwest across the water — they bypass the Sound entirely, so the protective mechanism isn't in play. By the same token, CAPE is far too low (well under the 1500 J/kg gate) for the sea-breeze convergence to amplify anything.

Most likely outcome. Bulk shear is 36 kt WSW, so any cell that does survive is being steered roughly to the east-northeast — away from Greenwich Cove. Expect at most a passing shower or a brief gusty period if a decaying cell drifts south. Surrounding towns north of I-95 will see more activity than the shoreline.

What would change the call. Watch the next radar frame for any cell tracking south-southeast (rather than ENE) and any RAP/HRRR refresh showing CAPE climbing past 500 J/kg. Neither is the current signal — but if both flipped, the call would move to high.

wind.regime_label: Light WSW gradient, fading near sunset

hourly.shift_note: slight build, peak of the window
gradient collapsing at sunset (20:30), direction goes random

wind.tactics_blurb: Ghosting WSW around 3–5 kt early, then collapsing near sunset. Stay right — pressure comes off the west shore first, and the CT side holes out worst.
wind.tactics_detail_html:
Regime call. Today is B-light (gradient-only, ghosting). Inland-vs-Sound deltaT is only 4.5°F (below the 5°F sea-breeze gate), the coastal SB ensemble is only 47% southerly with the LIS-west zone median sitting at WNW, and the gradient proxy is just 5 kt. Both sea-breeze ingredients are weak, so the day defaults to gradient — and the gradient itself is anemic. RYC is currently showing S 2.9 kt gusting 3.3 with a 10-min average of 2.2 kt.

Why right is favored. HRRR puts the evening flow at 245–256° (WSW) — that's classic channeled-down-the-Sound direction from the NYC corridor. Per the cove geography table, WSW gives steady direction but tends to hole out near the CT shore, with the better pressure mid-Sound. On the cove's race course that translates to more breeze on the right side looking upwind, with the left shore (Belle Haven / Field Point side) catching the worst of the shadows.

Shift character through the window. Direction holds in the WSW slot from 17:00 to 19:00 EDT — a steady shore-channeled flow, not oscillating. The numbers nudge up around 18:00 (peak ~5 kt) and ease back by 19:00. No model/obs divergence flag is firing today (low confidence on the correction, but `trust_obs_over_model` is false), so raw HRRR direction is good enough — there's no thermal to apply a maturation veer to.

Sunset collapse. HRRR fh=6 shows the surface flow falling apart by 20:00 EDT, backing to a token easterly at sub-1-kt speeds — roughly half an hour before astronomical sunset at 20:30. The extended outlook (fh=7–12) keeps speeds in the 0.4–3 kt range with no organized direction. If you're racing in this window, expect to finish in a drifter and plan starts around real-time pressure readings, not a forecast.

What racers will feel. Sub-raceable for any class above dinghies. Lasers and 420s can sail it but every shift matters; keelboats will struggle to make a course. Commit right early, get to mid-Sound pressure, and don't bank on the breeze holding past 19:30.

data_caveats: Buoy 44040 air observation is 76 min old — last reading 18:15Z.
Sea-breeze ensemble agreement is low (33% coastal southerly, LIS-west zone median WNW) — direction call is degraded relative to a clear thermal day.
